requirements.summary: Implement a responsive single-page layout with navbar, hero, about, services, testimonials, contact, and footer; enable in-page navigation to each section.
acceptanceCriteria: The home page renders all required sections in a logical order and is usable on mobile and desktop breakpoints.
Navigation allows jumping to each section (e.g., anchor links / scroll targets).
All copy is in English and uses clear placeholder business content (e.g., 'Your Business Name', 'hello@yourbusiness.com').

file_operations.description: Create the single-page brochure layout composing Navbar, Hero, About, Services (3+), Testimonials (3+), Contact, and Footer sections with semantic section IDs for anchor navigation (e.g., #about, #services, #reviews, #contact). Ensure responsive layout and spacing across breakpoints.

Add a top navigation bar with business name/logo area and anchor links to each section; include mobile-friendly behavior (e.g., collapsible menu). Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Sheet/Dropdown equivalents if available) to standardize interactions—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add hero section with headline, subheadline, primary call-to-action button that scrolls to Contact, and space for a hero image. Use shadcn-ui Button for the primary CTA—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add About section with English placeholder copy describing the business, mission, and value proposition with a clear H2 heading.
Add Services section listing at least three services with titles and short descriptions, designed responsively (grid on desktop, stacked on mobile). Service icon rendering will be wired in REQ-5.

Add Testimonials/Reviews section with at least three items (name, role/company placeholder, quote). Use shadcn-ui Card components if available for consistent styling—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add Contact section container with business email/phone/address placeholders and embed the contact form UI (form behavior completed in REQ-3).
Add footer with logo area, brief placeholder tagline, and social link placeholders (e.g., LinkedIn, X, Instagram) using accessible link text.
Ensure the React entry continues to render the App component and includes the global stylesheet import used for the site-wide theme.

requirements.summary: Create and apply a consistent warm-neutral visual theme (non blue/purple-dominant) across typography, spacing, buttons, and section backgrounds.
acceptanceCriteria: The site has a consistent design system (typography scale, spacing, and component styling) across all sections.
Primary and secondary actions have consistent button styling.
The primary color palette is not blue/purple-dominant.

file_operations.description: Define global theme tokens and base styles (CSS variables used by Tailwind/shadcn-ui color system), selecting a warm neutral primary palette (e.g., amber/stone/emerald-leaning) and avoiding blue/purple dominance. Add consistent typography defaults, section spacing conventions, and focus-visible styling.

Adjust Tailwind theme extensions only as needed to support the warm-neutral design system (e.g., container spacing defaults, typographic scale usage) while keeping the existing CSS-variable-based color setup.

Apply the design system consistently to hero layout: typographic scale, button variants, spacing, and background treatment. Use shadcn-ui Button variants for consistent styling—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Apply consistent card/panel styling, spacing, and headings aligned with the theme. Use shadcn-ui Card/Badge components if available—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure testimonial items match the theme via shared surface/background and typographic styling. Use shadcn-ui Card if available—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure footer uses the same palette, link styling, and spacing rules as the rest of the site, with clear hover/focus states.

requirements.summary: Add a contact form with client-side validation and a local non-backend submission confirmation state.
acceptanceCriteria: Form shows validation errors for empty required fields and invalid email format.
Submitting the form results in a clear user confirmation (e.g., 'Thanks—we will get back to you soon') without requiring any third-party service.
No backend calls are required for the contact form behavior.

file_operations.description: Implement the contact form UI with fields for name, email, and message; add client-side validation (required fields and email format) and a local success/thank-you state on submit (no backend calls). Use shadcn-ui Input/Textarea/Button components if available for consistent styling—verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire ContactForm into the Contact section and ensure the confirmation state is clearly visible after submission; keep business contact placeholders (email/phone/address) visible and accessible.

requirements.summary: Add SEO/sharing metadata and ensure accessible semantic structure, focus states, and sufficient color contrast.
acceptanceCriteria: The page has a meaningful title and meta description.
Headings follow a logical hierarchy (single H1, then H2s for sections).
Interactive elements are keyboard navigable with visible focus styling.

file_operations.description: Add/confirm a meaningful document title and include a meta description suitable for a generic small business brochure site; keep the document language set to English.

Ensure heading hierarchy: a single H1 in the hero, and H2 headings for each subsequent section. Confirm landmarks/semantics (nav, main, section, footer) and keyboard-friendly skip/jump behavior for in-page navigation.
Ensure visible focus styles for interactive elements (links, buttons, inputs) using focus-visible patterns; verify color contrast for text on all themed surfaces.

requirements.summary: Add the required generated images under frontend/public/assets/generated and integrate them into navbar/footer, hero, and services.
acceptanceCriteria: All required generated images exist under frontend/public/assets/generated.
The logo appears in the navbar and footer.
The hero image is visible in the hero section.
Service icons are displayed alongside service items.

file_operations.description: Add generated asset file at the exact path frontend/public/assets/generated/business-logo.dim_512x512.png so it can be referenced as a static image in the UI.
Add generated asset file at the exact path frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1600x900.png so it can be referenced as a static image in the hero section.
Add generated asset file at the exact path frontend/public/assets/generated/icon-consulting.dim_256x256.png for the Consulting service icon.
Add generated asset file at the exact path frontend/public/assets/generated/icon-design.dim_256x256.png for the Design service icon.
Add generated asset file at the exact path frontend/public/assets/generated/icon-support.dim_256x256.png for the Support service icon.
Render the logo image from frontend/public/assets/generated/business-logo.dim_512x512.png within the navbar with appropriate alt text and sizing.
Render the logo image from frontend/public/assets/generated/business-logo.dim_512x512.png within the footer with appropriate alt text and sizing.
Render the hero image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1600x900.png in the hero section (responsive sizing, decorative/meaningful alt text as appropriate).

Render service icons alongside their respective service items using: frontend/public/assets/generated/icon-consulting.dim_256x256.png, frontend/public/assets/generated/icon-design.dim_256x256.png, and frontend/public/assets/generated/icon-support.dim_256x256.png with accessible alt text.
